$source: CDC Travelers' Health country pages. See stages/01-data/references/cdc-raw.md.

$tier_model.required_vaccines: Entry requirements that apply regardless of origin country. Almost always empty for our 10 launch countries.
$tier_model.conditional_requirements: Entry requirements that apply only from certain origins (e.g. Yellow Fever if arriving from a YF-risk country). Each entry: {vaccine_id, condition}.
$tier_model.recommended_most_vaccines: CDC 'Recommended' without activity conditions. Applies to all travellers to this country.
$tier_model.recommended_some_vaccines: CDC 'Consider' or activity-conditional. Depends on itinerary, duration, activities.
$tier_model.routine_excluded: Routine vaccines (is_routine: true in vaccines.json) are not listed per country. Country pages reference the shared routine section.

$current_alerts_policy.purpose: Time-sensitive CDC travel health notices and current outbreak/flooding alerts. Rendered as a prominent banner on country pages, distinct from the stable health_notes field.
$current_alerts_policy.fields_per_alert: id (slug), title (short), detail (1-2 sentences), date_added (ISO), source (short phrase).
$current_alerts_policy.expiry_convention: Review every alert at least quarterly. Remove or refresh any alert older than 6 months unless confirmed still current on the CDC country page.

$entry_requirements_note: Visa and passport-validity data is sourced where verified; null means unverified — Stage 04 must add a 'check your government's guidance' note.

countries.name: Thailand

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older; includes airport transits over 12 hours.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in rural border provinces with Myanmar, Cambodia (except Buri Ram), and Malaysia. No risk in Bangkok, Chiang Mai, Chiang Rai, Phuket, Koh Samui, Koh Pha Ngan, or Krabi islands. Most city and resort travellers don't need malaria pills.

entry_requirements.visa_note: No visa required for US citizens staying 60 days or less.

countries.health_notes: Standard tropical travel precautions apply — food and water hygiene and mosquito-bite prevention are the main defences against the risks that are not vaccine-preventable.

current_alerts.title: Southern Thailand flooding
current_alerts.detail: CDC reports land travel may be dangerous in flood zones, with elevated risk of waterborne, vector-borne, and fungal diseases.

countries.name: India

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Travellers aged 9 months and older without a valid certificate can be detained for up to 6 days.

countries.malaria_note: Risk throughout the country, including Mumbai and New Delhi. No risk above 2,000 m, which covers parts of Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Sikkim, and higher areas of Kerala and Tamil Nadu. Most travellers to lowland India should take malaria pills.

entry_requirements.visa_note: All US citizens require a visa before arrival.

countries.health_notes: India has elevated chikungunya risk and widespread dog-transmitted rabies. CDC has flagged counterfeit ABHAYRAB rabies vaccine circulating in Delhi — only get rabies shots at reputable hospitals if exposed.

countries.name: Mexico
countries.malaria_note: Risk in parts of Campeche, Chiapas, and southern Chihuahua. Rare cases in Oaxaca, Sinaloa, Sonora, and Tabasco. No risk along the US border or in resort areas of the Yucatán coast.

entry_requirements.visa_note: No visa required for US citizens staying up to 180 days.

countries.health_notes: Food and water precautions matter — multidrug-resistant Salmonella has been identified in returning travellers. Dog rabies is present but not widespread.

current_alerts.title: Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever in northern Mexico
current_alerts.detail: Illness and death due to RMSF reported across Baja California, Sonora, Chihuahua, Coahuila, and Nuevo León. Use insect-bite precautions.

current_alerts.title: New World Screwworm Myiasis
current_alerts.detail: Cases reported across Mexico and Central America in areas where it had been eliminated. Avoid bug bites, keep open wounds clean and covered, and avoid time around livestock.

current_alerts.title: Multidrug-resistant Salmonella Newport
current_alerts.detail: Travellers returning from Mexico have been infected. Follow food and water precautions.

countries.name: Indonesia

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older.

countries.malaria_note: Risk across eastern Indonesia (Maluku, North Maluku, East Nusa Tenggara, Papua, West Papua — including Labuan Bajo and Komodo) and rural Kalimantan, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Lombok. No malaria risk in Jakarta, Bali resorts, Ubud, Gili Islands, or Java resort areas.
countries.health_notes: Dog-transmitted rabies is common across the country. Dengue and other mosquito-borne viruses are a year-round risk in the tropics.

current_alerts.title: Major flooding across Indonesian islands
current_alerts.detail: Land travel may be dangerous in flood zones and healthcare infrastructure has been damaged in affected areas.

current_alerts.title: Increased human rabies cases in West Timor
current_alerts.detail: CDC reports a rise in human rabies cases in West Timor. Avoid contact with dogs and other animals, and seek immediate care for any bite or scratch.

countries.name: Vietnam
countries.malaria_note: Rural areas only, with rare cases in the Mekong and Red River deltas. No malaria risk in Hanoi, Ho Chi Minh City, Da Nang, Hai Phong, Nha Trang, or Quy Nhon. Most tourist itineraries do not need malaria pills.
countries.health_notes: Dengue is a year-round risk across much of Vietnam, with larger outbreaks every 2–5 years. Mosquito-bite prevention matters even when a vaccine isn't advised. CDC does not currently advise the chikungunya vaccine for travel to Vietnam.

countries.name: Japan

entry_requirements.visa_note: No visa required for US citizens staying up to 90 days.

countries.health_notes: Japan has the lowest infectious-disease risk of our featured destinations. CDC lists Hepatitis A only for street-food enthusiasts or rural travellers, not for most city itineraries. Dog rabies is not common in Japan. Tick-borne encephalitis is present — avoid tick exposure if you'll be hiking, but CDC does not recommend the vaccine for travel to Japan.

countries.name: Kenya

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: Risk across the country below 2,500 m, including all safari parks. Rare cases in central urban Nairobi. Most travellers — including everyone on a safari itinerary — should take malaria pills. Seek medical attention for any fever during or within a year of travel.

entry_requirements.visa_note: US citizens require a visa before arrival.

countries.health_notes: CDC recommends yellow fever vaccine for most of Kenya. It is not advised if your travel is limited to Nairobi, the former North Eastern Province (Mandera, Wajir, Garissa), or most of the former Coast Province (except Taita-Taveta).

current_alerts.title: Active cholera transmission in specific counties
current_alerts.detail: CDC reports active cholera transmission in Garissa, Kisumu, Kwale, Migori, Mombasa, Nairobi, Narok, and Turkana counties. Cholera is rare in travellers; safe food, water, and handwashing remain the main protection.

countries.name: Egypt

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older; includes airport transits over 12 hours.

countries.health_notes: No malaria risk. Schistosomiasis is a real risk in the Nile and other fresh water — do not swim or wade in freshwater. Dog rabies is common; rabies shots may only be available at larger urban hospitals if you're exposed.

countries.name: Peru
countries.malaria_note: Risk across the country below 2,500 m east of the Andes, including Iquitos and Puerto Maldonado. Rare cases in Tumbes and Piura. No malaria risk in Lima, Arequipa, Cusco, Machu Picchu, Lake Titicaca, or the Pacific coast.
countries.health_notes: Yellow fever is recommended for travel to the Amazon regions below 2,300 m — Amazonas, Cusco, Huánuco, Junín, Loreto, Madre de Dios, Pasco, Puno, San Martín, Ucayali, and parts of the northern highlands. Not needed for trips limited to Lima, Cusco city, the Inca Trail, Machu Picchu, or the Pacific coast.

current_alerts.title: Oropouche cases in the Americas
current_alerts.detail: CDC reports low numbers of Oropouche cases across several countries in the region. Spread by midges and mosquitoes; take bite precautions.

current_alerts.title: Major flooding across Peru
current_alerts.detail: Multiple districts experiencing major flooding with elevated risk of waterborne disease including leptospirosis. Land travel may be dangerous in flood zones.

countries.name: Philippines

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older; includes airport transits over 12 hours.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in Palawan and the Mindanao island group. No risk in metropolitan Manila or other urban areas. Most tourist itineraries focused on Cebu, Bohol, or Metro Manila do not need malaria pills.
countries.health_notes: Dog rabies is common across the country. The Philippines FDA has warned of counterfeit Speeda rabies vaccine and Equirab antiserum in circulation — get rabies shots only at reputable hospitals if exposed. Cholera is presumed present; stick to safe food and water.

countries.name: Turkey

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: No active malaria transmission in Turkey. Historic risk in southeastern provinces is no longer considered significant by CDC.
countries.health_notes: Standard food and water precautions apply, especially outside large hotels. Stray dog populations are common in many cities, which is the main reason rabies may come up. West Nile virus and tick-borne diseases are present in summer; sensible mosquito and tick precautions matter if you spend time outdoors May to October.

countries.name: Brazil

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in the Amazon Basin states (Acre, Amapá, Amazonas, Maranhão, Mato Grosso, Pará, Rondônia, Roraima, and Tocantins). No risk in Rio de Janeiro, São Paulo, the eastern coastal beaches, or Iguaçu Falls.
countries.health_notes: Standard tropical travel precautions apply. Dengue, Zika, and chikungunya circulate year-round across much of Brazil; mosquito-bite prevention matters even where vaccines aren't advised.

countries.name: Morocco

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: No active malaria transmission in Morocco.
countries.health_notes: Standard food and water precautions apply, especially outside large hotels and riads. Stray dogs and animals are common in cities and rural areas, which is the main reason rabies may come up.

countries.name: United Arab Emirates

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: No active malaria transmission in the UAE.
countries.health_notes: Modern medical infrastructure; standard food and water precautions are sensible outside major hotels. Travellers continuing on to Saudi Arabia for Hajj or Umrah should also discuss meningococcal vaccination with their doctor.

countries.name: Tanzania

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission, including airport transits over 12 hours. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: Risk throughout the country below 1,800 metres, including all major safari parks (Serengeti, Ngorongoro, Tarangire, Lake Manyara) and Zanzibar. Most travellers should take malaria pills, and any fever during or within a year of travel needs medical attention.
countries.health_notes: Standard food and water precautions apply. Schistosomiasis is a risk in fresh water — avoid swimming in lakes and rivers including Lake Victoria.

countries.name: South Africa

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission, including airport transits over 12 hours. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in low-altitude areas of Limpopo and Mpumalanga (including Kruger National Park) and northeastern KwaZulu-Natal, mainly from October to May. No risk in Cape Town, the Garden Route, the Western Cape, or Johannesburg.
countries.health_notes: Standard food and water precautions apply. Tuberculosis and HIV are common in the general population — relevant context if you may need medical or dental care during your stay.

countries.name: Costa Rica

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older.

countries.malaria_note: Limited risk in some remote eastern lowland areas; most tourist itineraries (San José, Arenal, Manuel Antonio, Monteverde, Guanacaste beaches) do not need malaria pills.
countries.health_notes: Standard tropical travel precautions apply. Dengue circulates year-round across most of the country; mosquito-bite prevention is the main protection against the risks that aren't vaccine-preventable.

countries.name: Sri Lanka

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older.

countries.malaria_note: No active malaria transmission in Sri Lanka. WHO certified the country malaria-free in 2016.
countries.health_notes: Standard food and water precautions apply outside large hotels. Dengue is significant year-round, particularly during and after monsoon seasons; mosquito-bite prevention matters.

countries.name: Cambodia

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in rural and forested areas, especially near borders. No risk in Phnom Penh, Siem Reap, or the Angkor Wat temple complex. Most city and temple-focused itineraries don't need malaria pills.
countries.health_notes: Dog rabies is common in Cambodia. Dengue and Zika circulate; mosquito-bite prevention matters. Schistosomiasis, leptospirosis, and melioidosis are present in fresh water and floodwater.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Laos
countries.malaria_note: Risk in southern provinces (Attapeu, Champasak, Khammouane, Salavan, Savannakhet, Sekong). Rare in the north, and no risk in Luang Prabang or Vientiane.
countries.health_notes: Dog rabies is common across Laos. Dengue, Zika, and chikungunya circulate; mosquito-bite prevention matters. Schistosomiasis, melioidosis, and leptospirosis are present in fresh water.

current_alerts.title: Global Polio
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 2 notice for global polio. Adults previously vaccinated should consider a one-time booster before travel.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Myanmar

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in all areas, with chloroquine and mefloquine resistance documented. Most travellers outside major cities should take malaria pills.
countries.health_notes: Dog rabies is common in Myanmar. Dengue, Zika, leptospirosis, melioidosis, and tuberculosis are present. Standard food, water, and bug-bite precautions matter.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Nepal
countries.malaria_note: Active transmission in Sudurpashchim and Karnali provinces below 2,000 m. No risk in Kathmandu, Pokhara, or on typical Himalayan treks.
countries.health_notes: Dog rabies is common in Nepal. Dengue, leptospirosis, leishmaniasis, and tuberculosis are present. Standard food, water, and bug-bite precautions matter.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Bangladesh

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in the Chittagong Hill Tract districts (Bandarban, Khagrachari, Rangamati), parts of Chattogram, Cox's Bazar, Mymensingh, Kurigram, and Sylhet divisions. No risk in Dhaka.
countries.health_notes: Dog rabies is common in Bangladesh. Dengue is a year-round risk. Leptospirosis and melioidosis are concerns after heavy rain and flooding.

current_alerts.title: Global Dengue
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global dengue. Use mosquito-bite prevention day and night while in Bangladesh.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Singapore

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission.

countries.health_notes: Singapore has modern medical infrastructure and very low travel-illness risk. Dengue circulates year-round, so mosquito-bite prevention still matters. Leptospirosis, melioidosis, and tuberculosis are present.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Malaysia

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in rural and forested areas, primarily Plasmodium knowlesi. No risk in Kuala Lumpur, Penang State, or George Town.
countries.health_notes: Increased rabies cases reported in Sarawak. Dengue, Zika, leptospirosis, and tuberculosis are present. Standard food, water, animal-contact, and mosquito-bite precautions matter.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Maldives

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission.

countries.health_notes: Dengue and Zika are year-round mosquito-borne risks. Standard food and water precautions apply outside resort kitchens.

current_alerts.title: Global Dengue
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global dengue. Use mosquito-bite prevention day and night while in the Maldives.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: China
countries.malaria_note: Limited risk in some parts of China. Most city itineraries do not need malaria pills; check with a travel doctor if you plan rural travel.
countries.health_notes: Tap water is not drinkable in China, even in major cities. Dog rabies is common. Schistosomiasis is present in some freshwater areas.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: South Korea
countries.malaria_note: Limited March to December in rural northern parts of Incheon, Gangwon, and Gyeonggi provinces, including the demilitarized zone (DMZ). Only Plasmodium vivax. No risk in Seoul, Busan, or Jeju.
countries.health_notes: South Korea has modern medical infrastructure. Tick-borne encephalitis is present in some forested areas; tick precautions matter for hikers.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Taiwan
countries.health_notes: Dengue circulates seasonally; mosquito-bite prevention matters. Leptospirosis, melioidosis, and tuberculosis are present. Rabies is found in some wildlife, but infected dogs are uncommon.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Saudi Arabia

conditional_requirements.condition: Required for travellers performing Hajj or Umrah. Quadrivalent (ACWY) vaccine within 3–5 years and at least 10 days before arrival.
If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission.

countries.malaria_note: Risk limited to the Asir and Jazan regions near the Yemen border. No risk in Riyadh, Jeddah, Mecca, or Medina.
countries.health_notes: MERS can spread from camels and through respiratory droplets — avoid camel contact and unpasteurised camel products. Standard food and water precautions apply.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Jordan
countries.health_notes: Leishmaniasis spreads through sand fly bites. Leptospirosis, tuberculosis, and MERS are present. Standard food, water, and bug-bite precautions matter.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Israel
countries.health_notes: Poliovirus has been detected in environmental samples; adults previously vaccinated should consider a one-time booster. Rabies is present in dogs and wildlife. Leishmaniasis spreads through sand fly bites.

current_alerts.title: Global Polio
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 2 notice for global polio. Adults previously vaccinated should consider a one-time polio booster before travel.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Oman
countries.health_notes: Mosquitoes, ticks, and fleas can spread dengue, leishmaniasis, and Crimean-Congo hemorrhagic fever. Schistosomiasis is a risk in fresh water — avoid swimming in unchlorinated freshwater.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Qatar

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission.

countries.health_notes: Qatar has modern medical infrastructure. Standard food and water precautions are sensible outside major hotels.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: United States
countries.health_notes: CDC's destination page directs travellers to the US Health Alert Network for current public health issues. Make sure routine vaccines, including measles and flu, are up to date before travel.

countries.name: Canada
countries.health_notes: Rabies is present in terrestrial wildlife and bats. Tick and mosquito precautions are sensible for outdoor activities. Motor vehicle crashes are a leading cause of death for travellers — drive carefully.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Argentina
countries.health_notes: Yellow fever vaccine is recommended for travellers aged 9 months and older going to Corrientes and Misiones provinces (including Iguazú Falls). Dengue, Chagas disease, leptospirosis, and Zika are present.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Chile
countries.health_notes: Leptospirosis, Chagas disease, hantavirus, and tuberculosis are present. Use a repellent containing 20% or more DEET for outdoor travel.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Colombia
countries.malaria_note: Risk in areas below 1,700 m. No transmission in Bogotá, Cartagena, or Medellín.
countries.health_notes: Yellow fever vaccine is required for all arriving travellers aged 1 year and older, and CDC also recommends it for travellers aged 9 months and older going to most areas. Rabies is present in dogs and bats. Dengue, Zika, and chikungunya circulate year-round.

current_alerts.title: Yellow Fever cases increased in Colombia
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 2 notice for increased yellow fever cases. Make sure you have a current yellow fever vaccination at least 10 days before arrival.

current_alerts.title: Global Dengue
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global dengue. Use mosquito-bite prevention day and night while in Colombia.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Dominican Republic

conditional_requirements.condition: Required for travellers arriving from specific Brazilian states with yellow fever transmission risk (Espírito Santo, Minas Gerais, Rio de Janeiro, São Paulo).

countries.malaria_note: Risk in Azua, Elías Piña, La Altagracia, San Juan, and Santo Domingo provinces, plus parts of the capital. Punta Cana resort area is in La Altagracia — discuss with a travel doctor if visiting.
countries.health_notes: Dengue, Zika, and leishmaniasis are insect-borne risks. Schistosomiasis and leptospirosis are present in fresh water; avoid floodwater after hurricanes.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Jamaica
countries.health_notes: Dengue, Zika, and leptospirosis are concerns, especially after hurricane flooding. Avoid contact with floodwater and use mosquito-bite prevention.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Ecuador

conditional_requirements.condition: Required for travellers arriving from countries with risk of yellow fever transmission.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in lowland provinces below 1,500 m, including Cotopaxi, Esmeraldas, Morona-Santiago, Orellana, Pastaza, and Sucumbíos. No risk in Quito, Guayaquil, or the Galápagos Islands.
countries.health_notes: Yellow fever vaccine is recommended for travellers aged 9 months and older going to areas below 2,300 m east of the Andes. Dengue, Zika, Chagas disease, and leptospirosis are present. Rabies is in dogs and bats.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Nigeria

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older.

countries.malaria_note: Risk throughout Nigeria. CDC recommends prescription antimalarial medication for all travellers.
countries.health_notes: Lassa fever and mpox are present — avoid rodents and sick people. A diphtheria outbreak is active in several states; make sure your DTaP/Tdap is up to date.

current_alerts.title: Polio and Diphtheria in Nigeria
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 2 notice for polio and diphtheria in Nigeria. Make sure your routine polio and DTaP/Tdap vaccinations are up to date.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Ethiopia

conditional_requirements.condition: Required for travellers arriving from countries with risk of yellow fever transmission.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in all areas below 2,500 m. Chloroquine resistance documented; CDC recommends prescription antimalarial medication for all areas with risk.
countries.health_notes: Yellow fever is recommended for most travellers aged 9 months and older, except for travel limited to Afar or Somali regions. Schistosomiasis is a risk in fresh water. Rabies is present in dogs and bats.

current_alerts.title: Global Polio
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 2 notice for global polio. Adults previously vaccinated should consider a one-time polio booster before travel.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

current_alerts.title: Malaria outbreak in Ethiopia
current_alerts.detail: CDC reports a malaria outbreak across all 14 regions of Ethiopia. Take prescription antimalarial medication and use bite precautions.

countries.name: Ghana
countries.malaria_note: Risk throughout Ghana, primarily Plasmodium falciparum and chloroquine-resistant. CDC recommends prescription antimalarial medication for all travellers.
countries.health_notes: Yellow fever vaccine is required for all arriving travellers aged 9 months and older. Mpox (Clade II) is in active outbreak. Meningococcal disease may apply in the meningitis belt during the December–June dry season.

current_alerts.title: Clade II Mpox in Ghana and Liberia
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 2 notice for Clade II mpox in Ghana and Liberia. Vaccination is recommended for travellers anticipating certain higher-risk activities.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Uganda
countries.malaria_note: Risk throughout Uganda. CDC recommends prescription antimalarial medication for all travellers.
countries.health_notes: Yellow fever vaccine is required for all arriving travellers aged 1 year and older. Ebola and Marburg viruses are intermittent risks — avoid contact with sick people and infected animals. Schistosomiasis is a risk in fresh water.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Rwanda

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: Risk throughout Rwanda, primarily Plasmodium falciparum and chloroquine-resistant. CDC recommends prescription antimalarial medication for all travellers.
countries.health_notes: Dog rabies is common, and rabies vaccine for post-exposure care may only be available in larger urban hospitals. Standard food, water, and bug-bite precautions matter.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Zambia

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission.

countries.malaria_note: Risk throughout Zambia, primarily Plasmodium falciparum. CDC strongly recommends prescription antimalarial medication for all travellers.
countries.health_notes: Schistosomiasis is a risk in fresh water — avoid swimming in lakes and rivers. African sleeping sickness has been reported in some safari areas. Dengue and dog rabies are present.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

current_alerts.title: East African Sleeping Sickness
current_alerts.detail: CDC reports cases of African sleeping sickness among safari travellers. Use insect repellent and wear long sleeves and trousers in safari areas.

countries.name: Madagascar
countries.malaria_note: Risk in all areas except Antananarivo. Chloroquine resistance documented; primarily Plasmodium falciparum. CDC recommends prescription antimalarial medication for travel outside the capital.
countries.health_notes: Plague is present and can spread through flea bites and contact with infected animals or people. Avoid sick animals and people, and use bug-bite prevention.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.

countries.name: Australia
countries.health_notes: Australia has modern medical infrastructure and very low travel-vaccine needs. Dengue and Ross River virus are mosquito-borne risks, primarily in Queensland. Leptospirosis and melioidosis are present from water and soil contact. Tuberculosis is present.

current_alerts.title: Global Measles
current_alerts.detail: CDC has a Level 1 notice for global measles. Make sure you are fully vaccinated against measles before international travel.
